summary:
Odontogenic mutations affecting dental hard tissue are linked to defective proteins; thus, among provided options, PHEX appears most closely associated due to its role as a regulator involved indirectly through dentin composition and structure formation during development.

Agent1's reasoning (Round 2): After re-evaluating:

Key point from article [article-131837_10]: Mutations leading to deficiencies in tooth mineralizing tissues involve specific genetic factors influencing these processes.
Option analysis shows only one matches this context precisely—PHEX fits best because it affects bone matrix metabolism crucially impacting both skeletal systems including teeth via indirect regulation
